title: A Shark Bite Just Rewrote Federal Law
script: A Shark Attack Changed Law! In 2024, Lulu Gribbin was swimming off Walton County, Florida when a shark attacked — she lost her hand and part of her leg. Ninety minutes earlier, four miles away, another woman named Elisabeth Foley had already been bitten. Nobody warned Lulu. There was no way to. Now there is: President Trump just signed Lulu's Law, forcing the FCC to treat shark attacks like Amber Alerts — instant, geofenced warnings blasted straight to phones near the beach where it happened. Regulators have 180 days to get the system live. Lulu and Elisabeth both survived, rebuilt their lives, and pushed this through Congress themselves. So why did it take two bites to build one warning system?
